modules.title: Water – Analysis, Treatments and Industrial Applications
topics: Hardness and its units; determination of hardness by EDTA complexometric titration
Alkalinity determination and related numerical problems

modules.title: Boiler Problems and Softening Methods
topics: Boiler troubles: sludge and scale, priming and foaming, boiler corrosion, caustic embrittlement

modules.title: Lubricants and Lubrication
topics: Flash & fire points, cloud & pour points, aniline point, acid number, saponification number, steam emulsification number

modules.title: Polymers and Polymerisation
topics: Introduction to polymers and types of polymerisation
Classification and mechanism of polymerisation (free‑radical and ionic)
Preparation, properties and uses of PVC, PMMA, Teflon, Nylon‑6, Nylon‑6:6, polyester, phenol‑formaldehyde, urea‑formaldehyde, Buna‑N, Buna‑S, vulcanisation of rubber

modules.title: Phase Equilibrium and Corrosion
topics: Phase diagram of binary eutectic system (Cu‑Ag)

modules.title: Spectroscopic Techniques and Applications
topics: Principles, instrumentation and applications of electronic spectroscopy
Vibrational and rotational spectroscopy of diatomic molecules

modules.title: Periodic Properties
topics: Variation of s, p, d and f orbital energies across the periodic table

experiments: Water testing – determination of total hardness by complexometric titration, determination of mixed alkalinity (OH & CO₃, CO₃ & HCO₃), chloride ion estimation by argentometric method

Fuels & lubricant testing – flash & fire points determination (Pensky‑Martin, Abel’s, Cleveland’s open‑cup), calorific value by bomb calorimeter, viscosity and viscosity‑index determination (Redwood viscometers No.1 & No.2), proximate analysis of coal (moisture, ash, volatile matter, carbon residue), steam‑emulsification number and aniline point determination, cloud and pour point determination of lubricating oil

Alloy analysis – determination of % Fe in iron alloy by redox titration using N‑Phenyl anthranilic acid as internal indicator, determination of Cu and/or Cr in alloy by iodometric titration, determination of % purity of ferrous ammonium sulphate & copper sulphate

modules.title: DC Circuits
topics: Thevenin’s theorem and its application for analysis of series and parallel resistive circuits excited by independent voltage sources
Power and energy in DC circuits

modules.title: AC Circuits
topics: Average, RMS, form factor and peak factor of AC quantities
Analysis of R‑L, R‑C and R‑L‑C series and parallel circuits (1‑phase)
Necessity and advantages of three‑phase systems
Phase sequence and balanced/unbalanced supply and loads
Relationship between line and phase values for balanced star and delta connections
Power in balanced and unbalanced three‑phase systems and measurement techniques

modules.title: Magnetic Circuits & Single Phase Transformer
topics: Energy storage in linear magnetic systems
Magnetic field produced by a current‑carrying conductor
Induced voltage and laws of electromagnetic induction
General construction of a single‑phase transformer
Working principle and e.m.f. equation of a transformer

modules.title: Electrical Machines
topics: Construction, classification and working principle of DC machine
Construction, classification and working principle of induction machine
Construction, classification and working principle of synchronous machine
Working principle of three‑phase induction motor
Types of losses in electrical machines
Applications of DC, induction and synchronous machines

modules.title: Basic Electronics
topics: Number systems and conversion used in digital electronics
Common‑collector, common‑base and common‑emitter transistor configurations

experiments: Basic safety precautions; introduction and use of measuring instruments – voltmeter, ammeter, multimeter, oscilloscope; real‑life resistors, capacitors and inductors.

Measurement of steady‑state and transient time‑response of R‑L, R‑C and R‑L‑C circuits to a step change in voltage; sinusoidal steady‑state response of R‑L and R‑C circuits with impedance verification; observation of phase differences between current and voltage; resonance in R‑L‑C circuits.

Transformer experiments: observation of no‑load current waveform on an oscilloscope (non‑sinusoidal due to B‑H curve non‑linearity and harmonics); loading of a transformer with measurement of primary/secondary voltages, currents and power.

Determination of equivalent‑circuit parameters of a single‑phase transformer by open‑circuit and short‑circuit tests; estimation of voltage regulation and efficiency at various loads; verification by load test.

Demonstration of cut‑out sections of machines: DC machine (commutator‑brush arrangement), induction machine (squirrel‑cage rotor), synchronous machine (field winding‑slip‑ring arrangement) and single‑phase induction machine.
Torque‑speed characteristic measurement of a separately excited DC motor.

Synchronous speed determination of two‑ and four‑pole three‑phase induction motors; direction reversal by changing phase‑sequence; torque‑slip characteristic of induction motor; generator operation of an induction machine driven at super‑synchronous speed.
Synchronous machine operation as a generator: stand‑alone operation with load; voltage control through field excitation.
Study of V‑I characteristics of diodes.
Verification of diode applications.
Transistor applications as amplifier and switch.
Verification of truth tables for various logic gates and flip‑flops.
Realisation of various gates and flip‑flops.
Verification of De Morgan’s theorems.

Drawing sectional elevations from foundation to ceiling

Fitting shop (8 hours)

subjects.subject_name: Swachh Bharat Summer Internship / Unnat Bharat Abhiyan (100 Hrs) / Rural Outreach

modules.title: Wave nature of particles and the Schrödinger equation
topics: Group velocity (v_g) and phase velocity (v_p) relations

modules.title: Wave optics
topics: Interference of light: Young’s double‑slit experiment, Newton’s rings, Michelson interferometer, Mach‑Zehnder interferometer
Fraunhofer diffraction from a single slit and a circular aperture
Rayleigh criterion for resolution and its application to vision
Diffraction gratings and their resolving power

topics: Fermi level for intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors

topics: Electric field and electrostatic potential for various charge distributions

experiments: Determine the dispersive power of a prism.
Determine the wavelength (λ) of sodium light using Newton’s rings.
Measure the resolving power of a telescope.
Young’s double‑slit experiment (YDSE).
Determine the frequency of AC mains supply.
Measure the I‑V characteristics of a p‑n junction diode.
Determine the wavelength (λ) of a diode using single‑slit diffraction.
Determine Planck’s constant using a photocell.
Hall‑effect experiment.
Calibrate an ammeter using a reference Zener diode.
Study the temperature dependence of reverse saturation current in a p‑n junction diode and determine the energy band gap.
Determine the wavelength (λ) of sodium light using a plane diffraction grating.
Determine the prominent spectral lines of a mercury source with a plane diffraction grating.
Determine the numerical aperture of an optical fiber.
Determine the wavelength (λ) of a given laser using a plane diffraction grating.

topics: Composition of cast iron and carbon steels and the iron‑carbon diagram

Tensile test – stress‑strain diagram of ductile and brittle materials
Hooke’s law and modulus of elasticity

experiments: Study of Universal Testing Machines

Study of Lathe Machine
Study of Drilling Machines

modules.title: UNIT I – Computer Fundamentals
topics: Computer organization: CPU, registers, bus architecture, instruction set
Computer applications in e‑Business, bio‑informatics, health care, remote sensing & GIS, meteorology and climatology

modules.title: UNIT II – Algorithms, Programming Fundamentals and C++ Basics
topics: Introduction to algorithms, complexity analysis and flowcharts
Fundamentals of programming and categories of programming languages
Program design and programming paradigms (procedural vs. object‑oriented)
Concepts of OOP: characteristics and principles
Introduction to C++: character set, tokens, precedence and associativity
C++ program structure, data types, variables, operators, expressions and statements
Control structures, I/O operations, arrays and functions

topics: Objects and classes, scope resolution operator

topics: Computer networking fundamentals: goals, ISO‑OSI model and layer functions
Internetworking concepts, networking devices, TCP/IP model

Security measures: firewalls, best practices, computer ethics
Overview of cyber laws and internet fraud prevention

Data models, DBMS architecture and data independence
Data dictionary, DBA responsibilities, primary keys
Data definition language (DDL) and data manipulation language (DML)
Cloud computing concepts: definition, cloud infrastructure
Advantages and disadvantages of cloud computing

experiments: Study and practice of internal & external DOS commands

Creation and editing of Power‑Point slides using MS‑PowerPoint
Creation and manipulation of database tables using SQL in MS‑Access

Write a program to illustrate arrays
Write a program to illustrate functions

modules.title: Language Laboratory Sessions
topics: Public Speaking and oral skills with emphasis on conversational practice, extempore speech, JAM (Just a minute sessions), describing objects and situations, giving directions, debate, telephonic etiquette.
Reading Comprehension: Intensive reading skills, rapid reading, and reading aloud (Reading material to be selected by the teacher).
Writing a book review. Standard text must be selected by the teacher.
Role plays: preparation and delivery of topics selected by teacher/faculty.
